treatment_information.specific_treatment: carbohydrate loading with intravenous dextrose and oral carbohydrates

treatment_timeline.event: first attack managed as appendicitis with emergency laparoscopic exploration
second attack managed as sinus tachycardia with beta adrenergic blockers
third attack managed conservatively for questionable renal colic
fourth attack complicated with transient hyponatremia and high serum creatinine levels, managed as interstitial nephritis
fifth attack managed as partial intestinal obstruction with diagnostic laparoscopy
sixth attack raised suspicion of acute porphyria, positive Watson and Schwartz test for urinary porphobilinogen
genetic studies confirmed HMBS gene mutation

patient_outcome.final_status: improved symptoms over 6 days, managed as out-patient
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: followed up at the clinic with regular renal functions, hemoglobin and blood pressure monitoring
